title: Humboltia ligulata
taxonomy: Humboltia ligulata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It belongs to the genus Humboltia, which is a group of plants that are native to tropical regions. The species Humboltia ligulata is further divided into different varieties and subspecies based on its distinct characteristics. Within the Apocynaceae family, Humboltia ligulata is part of the larger clade of dogbanes and oleanders. The classification of Humboltia ligulata is based on its morphological and molecular features.
uses: Humboldtia ligulata is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research suggests that extracts from the plant may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. Traditional medicine practitioners in certain regions have used Humboldtia ligulata to treat various health conditions, including fever and digestive issues. Further research is essential to determine the safe and effective use of Humboldtia ligulata as a medicinal plant.

side_effects: Humboldtia ligulata, a plant species native to Southeast Asia, has been reported to have potential side effects due to its toxic properties. It contains a group of compounds called homobaslanolides and glycosides that can be toxic if ingested. intro: Humboldtia ligulata, also known as the 'Kanukal', is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka and India, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Humboldtia ligulata include flavonoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Humboldtia ligulata is commonly prepared as a decoction or a paste, and is also used to make herbal teas and infusions, which are valued for their therapeutic and medicinal benefits.
